french-onion-soup.name: French Onion Soup
procedure: Cut up all the onions
In pan with butter, oil & salt
Stir for at least an hour, or until brown
Throw in bay leaves and deglaze with wine
Pour in some flour and stir
Worcestershire sauce, rosemary & stock, 30 minutes
Put bread with cheese in oven; serve with soup
notes: To make it vegan, swap with vegan worcestershire sauze and vegan cheese
Recommended to use vegetable stock
For scaling: use multiple pans to sauté onions, throw in one big pot

hungarian-goulash.name: Hungarian Goulash
procedure: Advised to cup everything up beforehand
Sauté meat in butter with salt, not too much at the sate time
Add the onions, sauté for a few minutes
Add the garlic, one minute
Add the vegetables (bell pepper, carrot, tomato), few minutes
Stir in tomato paste, then add caraway seeds and paprika powder
Add the bay leaves and stock, let it simmer for 2 hours
Throw in the potatoes, simmer for at least an hour more
Serve with some bread and sour cream
notes: The home-made beef stock can be substituted by a jar of store-bought beef buillon (~300ml), with extra water
Sauté everything in batches. Especially for the meat, make sure the pan is not too crowded. Transfer everything to a big pot after

bianco-mangiare.name: Bianco Mangiare
procedure: Mix sugar, corn starch, salt and half the almond milk until slurry
Pour in the rest of the almond milk, the lemon peel and cinnamon stick
Bring to a simmer while stirring
When a bit less thick than yoghurt, pour in a mold and refrigerate
Optional: serve with almond sprinkles
notes: The almond sprinkles are optional, but make for a nice plate

white-chocolate-cookies.name: White Chocolate Cookies
procedure: Chop up the white chocolate, and potentially the oat flakes
In a bowl, mix the flour, salt, baking powder & baking soda
In another bowl, mix butter, vanilla extract and both sugars
To the last bowl, add the eggs one by one, keep mixing
When smooth, mix the two bowls together
Add in the oat flakes, white chocolate and cranberries
Let the mixture cool down 15 minutes & preheat the oven to 170 degrees Celsius
Roll into balls of ~4cm width, and flatten them a bit
Put on a tray, and in the oven for 10m30s
Let each batch settle outside the oven for half an hour
notes: The cooking time highly depends on the batch size per oven run
If the oat flakes are too coarse, chop them up

naan.name: Naan
procedure: Mix water & sugar
Mix in the dry yeast, sit for 15 minutes
In a separate bowl, mix flour, salt, yogurt & baking powder
Mix both, and knead well
Cover bowl with a towel, let it sit in a warm place for 90 minutes
Make even balls, and roll out well
Bake in a hot pan with oil
Put finely chopped garlic & butter in microwave for 30 seconds
Spread garlic & butter with some cilantro over the naan
notes: To make vegan: use vegan yoghurt

red-lentil-curry.name: Red Lentil Curry
procedure: Rinse lentils, chop garlic & chilis, grate ginger & turmeric
Mix garam masala, black pepper, salt, cumin, coriander, chili and curry powders
Sauté garlic, ginger, turmeric and peppers
Add spice mix
Add stock, bring to a simmer
Add the lentils and tomatoes, simmer for 30 minutes
Chop spinach & add, 10 more minutes
Add coconut milk, lemon juice and peanut butter, 10 more minutes
notes: Peanut butter is quite tough, so water down a bit and mix well
Of course, use veggie stock

roti-chicken-masala.name: Roti Chicken Masala
procedure: Cut up onions, garlic, chicken, potatoes and kouseband (if not frozen)
Sauté onion, then garlic
Add the chicken, until outer layer is done
Add tomato paste and stir for two minutes
Add the masala mix, stir for a minute
Add the potatoes, stir for two minutes
Add the stock, cover the lid and simmer for 45 minutes
Optionally: cut the head of a Madame Jeanette pepper, and add to stew
Add the kouseband, simmer for some 15 minutes more
Cook the eggs and heat up the roti
Serve the stew with eggs, roti and optionally yellow sambal
notes: I prefer chicken thighs for their natural fat contents
Madame Jeanette is a spicy pepper, use with caution (or use multiple)
Fresh kouseband is expensive, frozen tend to do well and are cheaper
Store-bought 'Hindoestaanse kerrie masala' will suffice, you might make it yourself
Making roti (the pancake) is labor intensive, store-bought roti dahl (with yellor split peas) are delicious
Sambal made from yellow/Suriname/Madame Jeanette peppers goes with will this dish

veggie-stock.name: Veggie Stock
procedure: Cut op all the veggies
Sauté in a big soup pot for 10 minutes, stir often
Throw in tomato paste, stir for one more minute
Add salt, black pepper corns, MSG and Italian herbs, stir
Add water and simmer for 2 hours
notes: About the actual cutting of the veggies: rough pieces
Boil water first for maximum efficiency
MSG is not necessary if you don't have it
Italian herbs? Just throw in whatever

chili-con-carne.name: Chili Con Carne
procedure: Cut up the onion, garlic and beller peppers, keep separate
Either cut up the chipotle peppers (in adobe sauce), or use a mixer
Cook the minced meat in butter
When brown-ish, throw in the onion, few minutes, garlic
Throw in the veggies, stir well
Add tomato paste, stir for a minute, add spices, stir well
Add the crushed tomatoes and chipotle peppers in adobe sauce
Add all the beans, bring to a simmer and stir occasionally for an hour
In the meanwhile: cook rice and serve together
Optionally, serve with crème fraîche, nachos and guacamole dip
ingredients: can chipotle peppers in adobe sauce
notes: Optionally: add red wine & cocoa powder before crushed tomatoes
If making a big batch, cook everything in batches as well, add to big pot
Making a big batch adds to the total cooking time

brownies.name: Brownies
procedure: Prepare the bain-marie; bring water to a simmer
Cut the chocolate and butter, add to bain-marie and melt while mixing
When completely molten: cool a bit
Preheat the oven to 180C
In another bowl, mix sugars, add one egg at a time and properly mix
Add the salt, vanilla essence and chocolate/butter mix, mix everything well
Sift in the flour and cocoa powder, fold in carefully
To an oven tray, add parchment paper, and a bit of liquid butter or oil
Pour in the batter and bake for 32 minutes
Leave in the fridge overnight, cut up and serve
notes: First let the product cool at room temperature, then put in the fridge
These measures work for my oven and baking tray, scale by doing multiple batches

beef-stew.name: Beef Stew
procedure: Cut the beef chuck, fry in butter for 8 mins per batch
After each batch of food, throw it in one big pot, and deglaze with red wine
In the meanwhile, cut the onions, carrots, selery and garlic
Fry the mire poix for 5 minutes, add garlic, 1 more minute
Cut the mushrooms. Fry for 5 minutes, throw in some flour (opt.), 1 more minute
Add to the big pot: red wine, tomato can, stock, herbs, spices and Worcestershire sauce
Simmer for at least 1,5 hours
Cut and add potatoes, simmer 30 more minutes
Add frozen green peas, simmer 20 more minutes
Optionally serve with fresh parsley
notes: Leave plenty of space for the beef chuck to fry, otherwise you'll boil it
For Dutchies, buy 'runder riblappen'
Mire poix = fancy name for onions, carrot & selery

one-pot-wonder.name: One Pot Wonder
procedure: Cut the onions into half-slices, set aside
Cut the chicken into bite-sized, set aside
Cut the courgette and bell peppers, set aside
Fry the bacon bits with the onions, stir for 2 minutes
During the stirring below, drain liquid from the legume containers
Add the chicken, stir for 4 minutes
Add the courgette and bell peppers, stir for 2 minutes
Add tomato paste, stir for 2 minutes
Add the smoked paprika powder and cumin, stir for 3 minutes
Add the can of tomatoes, and legumes, stir for 3 minutes
Let the pot simmer for 30 minutes
In the meanwhile, cook rice
Add the soy sauces and spinache, stir well for 4 minutes
Let the pot simmer for some 15 more minutes
Serve with rice
notes: It is possible to use potatoes instead of rice, cut them up and throw in with the sauces & spinache. Simmer for 20 minutes more

bobotie.name: Bobotie
procedure: Cut the onions, carrot, garlic and chili, grate the ginger
Sauté the onions and carrot for 2 minutes, add the garlic, chili and ginger for 2 minutes
Add the minced beef/lamb/whatever, stir often. In the meanwhile: cut the courgette and green beans
If a bit of a color: add the courgette and green beans
After few minutes, add the spices and fry for few minutes as well
In the meanwhile, soak the cashews for a minute and cut in halves
Preheat the oven to 170C
Add the cashews, jam/chutney, and bay leaves
After a minute, add water and cook for 10 minutes
In the meanwhile, mix the eggs, milk and soy sauce (custard)
Transfer cooked stuff to an oven dish, layer the custard on top
Bake for 30 minutes (or until set). Meanwhile: cook rice, serve together
notes: Can by any mince, I heard lamb and beef are nice
Rice could also be nice yellow rice
